TALK WITH


STRAHORN

GO TO HIS OFFlCES-COUN
WILL INSIST ON UNION


DEPOT AGREEMENT.

pages.raw_text:
For the last few days some MEM
bers of the city council favorable
to the passage of the railway fran
chises have been in conference
with Robert E. Strahorn, president
of the North Coast railway, at his
offices in the Paulsen building.


The ostensible purpose was to
talk over matters of grade separa-
tion and other phases of the fran.
chises, and the excuse was offered
that this work could be done bet
ter there than elsewhere, because
of all the plats, maps and charts
being at Strahorn's office.


One member of the city council
favorable to the passage OF the
franchise, who was invited to tho
Strahorn conferences, refused to
go. on the ground that If Mr.. Stra.
norm had any proposals to make
to the city the proper place to pre-
sent them was on the floor Of tho
city council.


do you think there is deal on
foot to knock out the union depot
and a lot of other things?" was
asked this councilman.


"Oh, | think not They will
never get the franchise with my
vote II this is done. There are
several members of the city coun-
Gil just like l am, who are willing
that the franchises shall be grant.
ed, but want square deal for the
people at large. l want a union
depot, a common user clause and
adequate grade separation: with
out these things the franchises
shall never receive my vote. And
| think that least one-half Of the
city council are about of the same
mind"
